evals.name: standard-audit
evals.prompt: run a security audit on this project
evals.expected_output: Full v2.2 security audit report with 6-step progress, positive-first structure, severity-tagged issues, and script-computed Audit Details

step_1_discover: Domain detected as swayzi.co (not yourdomain.com or unknown)
Step 1 done message includes framework + ORM + route count + domain
step_2_select: Test selection summary shows total OWASP test cases
Shows how many test types will run (script checks)
Shows how many skipped with reason
step_3_scan: TOTALS pass + warn + fail = total checks
TOTALS pass count matches actual PASS rows
TOTALS warn count matches actual WARN rows
TOTALS fail count matches actual FAIL rows
AUDIT_DETAILS category rows sum to same total as TOTALS
No NOT IMPLEMENTED FAIL rows in output
Step 3 done message includes total checks and pass/warn/fail breakdown
step_4_analyze: Profile judgment calls section is read
Route files are read for judgment analysis
Judgment results are NOT printed as a separate list
Step 4 done message shows number of judgment calls completed
step_5_report_structure: Report starts with header including date and profile name
Project Profile table present with framework, ORM, auth, hosting, domain
What's secure section appears BEFORE Executive Summary
What's secure includes categories that pass via LLM judgment (IDOR, privilege escalation)
What's secure is generous — includes categories with minor WARNs
Executive Summary present with posture emoji
Executive Summary shows exact pass/warn/fail numbers from TOTALS
Executive Summary shows distinct findings count and affected categories count
What needs attention section present with issues grouped by category
Audit Details table at end copies verbatim from scan.sh AUDIT_DETAILS output
Skip count and defer count summary lines present at end
step_5_report_issues: Issues have severity tags: red circle for FIX, yellow for INVESTIGATE, green for MONITOR/ACCEPT
Issues are ordered by priority (FIX categories first, then INVESTIGATE, then MONITOR/ACCEPT)
Each FIX/INVESTIGATE issue has all 7 sections: WSTG Reference, Threat, What's wrong, What could happen, What could go wrong when fixing, How others solve this, Recommended action
What could go wrong when fixing names specific risks (not generic statements)
Related findings are grouped (e.g., 9 rate limiting WARNs = 1 issue)
Unrelated findings are NOT grouped together
False positives noted as ACCEPT with brief explanation (not full 7-section blocks)
No raw 110-row Findings tables in report body
step_5_report_correctness: WSTG IDs are correct: ATHZ-02 (not ATHZ-01), INPV-11 (not INPV-03), INPV-20 (not INPV-06), DOS-03 (not DOS-02), PRIV-01 (not IDNT-01)
Audit Details table numbers match the script-computed table exactly
Issue count in Executive Summary matches actual number of issue blocks written
Category count in Executive Summary matches number of category headings in What needs attention
step_6_close: Reminder about /security-review for deep code analysis
Offers to save report to file

evals.name: specific-concern
evals.prompt: I'm about to launch this app publicly. Can you check if there are any security issues with my API routes and whether my production headers are set up correctly?
evals.expected_output: Full audit covering ALL categories despite user asking about a subset

completeness: discover.sh runs and detects nextjs-prisma profile
Full audit covers ALL categories, not just API routes and headers
What's secure section includes non-API categories (code injection, SSRF, error handling)
Issues cover infrastructure config, privacy/legal, logging — not just what user asked about
format: Audit Details table present with correct numbers
No raw Findings tables in body

evals.name: minimal-prompt
evals.expected_output: Skill triggers and runs full audit despite minimal prompt

triggering: The skill triggers — discover.sh is invoked
scan.sh runs and produces TOTALS line
